name: globus-compute-common

metadata.name: globus-compute-common
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Common tools for Globus Compute projects

documentation:
[![pre-commit.ci status](https://results.pre-commit.ci/badge/github/globus/globus-compute-common/main.svg)](https://results.pre-commit.ci/latest/github/globus/globus-compute-common/main)

# globus-compute-common

This package contains common utilities for use across various Globus Compute projects.

For example, constants shared between the Globus Compute Web Service and the SDK are
defined here (and may be re-exported by the SDK).

classes.name: globus_compute_common.task_storage.redis.ImplicitRedisStorage
documentation:
This storage adapter stores task data to redis only.

It is named "implicit" because it assumes that the task object itself is some form
of RedisTask which will write to redis either on setattr or some save/commit step.

classes.name: globus_compute_common.task_storage.base.TaskStorage

methods.name: globus_compute_common.task_storage.base.TaskStorage.store_result
documentation:
Store the result of a task.
If the storage call succeeded, set reference to data in Task.result_reference
If the storage failed, raises StorageException

methods.name: globus_compute_common.task_storage.base.TaskStorage.get_result
documentation:
Get the result of a task.
Returns a string if a result was found, and None otherwise.
Raises a storage exception if retrieval failed

methods.name: globus_compute_common.task_storage.base.TaskStorage.store_payload
documentation:
Store the payload of a task.
If the storage call succeeded, set reference to data in Task.payload_reference
If the storage failed, raises StorageException

methods.name: globus_compute_common.task_storage.base.TaskStorage.get_payload
documentation:
Get the task payload.
Returns a string if a payload was found, and None otherwise.
Raises a storage exception if retrieval failed
Abstract class which defines the interface for task storage.

modules.name: globus_compute_common.globus_compute_flake8
documentation:
A Flake8 Plugin for use in Globus Compute

Catches log calls which do eager string formatting and imports of the `typing`
module which don't match our preferred form.
type: ignore

classes.name: globus_compute_common.redis_task.RedisTask

methods.name: globus_compute_common.redis_task.RedisTask.__init__
documentation:
If optional values are passed, then they will be written.

Otherwise, they will fetched from any existing task entry.
\:param redis_client: Redis client for properties to get/set
\:param task_id: UUID of the task, as str
\:param user_id: ID of user to whom this task belongs
\:param function_id: UUID of the function for this task, as str
\:param container: UUID of container in which to run, as str
\:param payload: serialized function + input data
\:param task_group_id: UUID of task group that this task belongs to
\:param queue_name: name of AMQP queue where results will be sent
\:param endpoint_id: UUID of the endpoint the task was sent to

methods.name: globus_compute_common.redis_task.RedisTask.ttl
documentation: Expires task after expiration time, if not already set.

methods.name: globus_compute_common.redis_task.RedisTask.delete
documentation: Removes this task from Redis, to be used after the result is gotten

methods.name: globus_compute_common.redis_task.RedisTask.exists
documentation: Check if a given task_id exists in Redis

methods.name: globus_compute_common.redis_task.RedisTask.load
documentation: Load a task from storage. Raises a ValueError if the task is not found.

ORM-esque class to wrap access to properties of tasks.

Creation:
  Create a new task by instantiating this class, e.g.
  >>> RedisTask(redis_client, "foo_id")

Loading:
  Read a task from storage using the `load()` classmethod, e.g.
  >>> RedisTask.load(redis_client, "foo_id")

Creation and Loading will each check `RedisTask.exists` when invoked. If the task_id
already exists on creation, or does not exist on load, a ValueError will be raised.
For example:
  >>> RedisTask.load(redis_client, "foo_id")
  Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
  ValueError: Cannot load task foo_id: does not exist
  >>> RedisTask(redis_client, "foo_id")  # ok
  >>> RedisTask(redis_client, "foo_id")
  Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
  ValueError: Conflict. Cannot create task foo_id: already exists

This class provides various task fields as descriptors via RedisField, and is
responsible for de/serializing various data from/to hstore in Redis.

There are several elements of this pattern of use which need to be fixed. It is
important to be aware of the following:
- there is currently no use of Redis transactions, so nothing is atomic
- Each time a field descriptor is accessed, it is read, returned, and discarded.
  Reading a field multiple times, even in a single python statement, is vulnerable
  to data races
- no field requirements or validity are enforced -- reading a field can raise an
  error if bad data were written to Redis
- each field is read individually, which can be inefficient and inconsistent (vs
  getall or setall semantics)

documentation: Removes this lock from Redis, not used for initial expiring case

documentation: Check if a given lock exists

methods.name: globus_compute_common.redis_endpoint_lock.RedisEndpointLock.load
documentation: Returns an object lock if it exists, otherwise raises

ORM-esque class to wrap access to a lock on an endpoint UUID

Creation:
  Create a lock by instantiating this class, e.g.
  >>> RedisEndpointLock(redis_client, "e_uuid_12345", 1665605117.077466)

Finding:
  Returns a lock dict if the lock exists for the endpoint
  >>> RedisEndpointLock.find(redis_client, "e_uuid_12345")
  This will return None if there was no lock

Checking for lock:
  Method that returns True if there exists a lock *now* for
  the specified endpoint UUID
  >>> RedisEndpointLock.exists(redis_client, "e_uuid_12345")

This class provides various task fields as descriptors via RedisField, and is
responsible for de/serializing various data from/to hstore in Redis.

modules.name: globus_compute_common.testing
documentation: This module defines tools for helping to test funcx_common.

modules.name: globus_compute_common.sdk_version_sharing
documentation:
The globus-compute-sdk passes a user-agent header string containing sdk version
information to the web service, which logs that information. This is a small,
centralized compatibility module for that behavior.

classes.name: globus_compute_common.redis.serde.ComputeRedisSerde
documentation:
A Serializer/Deserializer for data going into or out of Redis.

The base implementation just stringifies data and does nothing when
deserializing.

classes.name: globus_compute_common.redis.fields.RedisField

methods.name: globus_compute_common.redis.fields.RedisField.__init__
documentation: TODO: have a cache and TTL on the properties so that we aren't making so many
redis gets?

Descriptor class that stores data in redis.

Uses owning class's redis client in `owner.redis_client` to connect, and uses
owner's hname in `owner.hname` to uniquely identify the keys.

Fields can be serialized and deserialized by setting a ComputeRedisSerde.

classes.name: globus_compute_common.redis.fields.HasRedisFieldsMeta

methods.name: globus_compute_common.redis.fields.HasRedisFieldsMeta.__new__
documentation: don't type check __new__ -- metaclasses are hard for mypy

This metaclass should be used by any class which has RedisFields included.
It is also provided via the HasRedisFields class for use in simple
inheritance, for convenience.

This inspects all class attributes and sets the keys on RedisField
attributes to be the same as their attribute name.

functions.name: globus_compute_common.redis.connection.default_redis_connection_factory
documentation:
Construct a Redis client for a given redis URL.
If no URL is given, the COMPUTE_COMMON_REDIS_URL environment variable will be used.

If no URL is given and the environment variable is not populated,

  redis://localhost:6379

will be used as the default.

methods.name: globus_compute_common.redis.pubsub.ComputeRedisPubSub.republish_from_queue

Tasks pushed to Redis pubsub channels might have gone unreceived.
When a new endpoint registers, it should republish tasks from it's queues
to the pubsub channels.

methods.name: globus_compute_common.redis.pubsub.ComputeRedisPubSub.get
documentation:
---
param timeout: wait time for getting a message, in milliseconds
type timeout: int
---

methods.name: globus_compute_common.redis.pubsub.ComputeRedisPubSub.get_final_messages
documentation:
Yield back messages via `get()` for as long as the pubsub is marked
as subscribed.

This class provides a layer over the Redis lib's `PubSub` functionality to
push and pop messages into an endpoint_id-specific queue.
Publishing and getting messages (plus subscribe/unsubscribe) are used to
send messages via a pubsub channel in the normal case.

If there is no recipient listening for a message, it is pushed into a queue instead.
Subscribing pops all messages from the queue and puts them onto the pubsub channel.

**IMPORTANT**

Unsubscribing from a redis channel is not a synchronous operation. When
unsubscribing, ensure clean teardown by calling `get_final_messages()`.

modules.name: globus_compute_common.messagepack.protocol
documentation:
The python protocol definition.

A MessagePackProtocol only needs to define two actions:
  - pack()
  - unpack()

And the two must be inverses on any valid inputs.

methods.name: globus_compute_common.messagepack.protocol.MessagePackProtocol.pack
documentation: Pack a message into bytes.

methods.name: globus_compute_common.messagepack.protocol.MessagePackProtocol.unpack
documentation: Unpack bytes into a message.

methods.name: globus_compute_common.messagepack.packer.MessagePacker.detect_protocol_version
documentation: read the first byte of the buffer and decode it

modules.name: globus_compute_common.messagepack.protocol_versions.proto1
documentation:
This file defines Protocol Version 1 of the funcx messagepack protocol.

v1 of the protocol does the following:
- each message is encoded as a JSON object in UTF-8 with a one-byte header, no newlines
- the first byte of the header contains the protocol version, always 1
- the JSON body contains exactly two fields: "message_type" (a string) and "data" (an object)
- "message_type" determines which class is used to load the content in "data"

For example, under protocol v1, a heartbeat request message could be formulated as

    {"message_type":"heartbeat_req","data":{}}

where `` is the version byte.

A Task message can be formulated with

    {"message_type":"task","data":{"task_id": "359e2ec3-caef-4258-a149-a67767af0ee8","container_id":"ee138b83-fd62-4b57-b31b-55e460569fd2"}}

== Multi-Message Payloads

Because newlines are a forbidden character, multiple messages may be newline-delimited, as in

    {"message_type":"foo","data":{}}
    {"message_type":"bar","data":{}}

== Unknown Field Handling (loading)

The protocol defines the following behavior for missing and unknown fields:

  - if a payload does not contain a required field, loading will fail with a
    pydantic ValidationError

  - if a payload defines fields which are not included in the current message
    definitions, they are ignored and a warning is logged

  - if an envelope includes unknown fields (other than "message_type" and "data"), they
    are ignored and a warning is logged

Therefore, new fields may be added to the message classes, but they must not be
required until all messages contain those fields.
